page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7.8xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 architecture, utilizing Intel Xeon 8369B processors operating at a base frequency of 2.7 GHz. The instance is configured with 256.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 16 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place this instance in the average tier for most compute and memory tasks, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 13384.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 35326.14. Its memory subsystem achieves a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 1689.81 GB/sec and a large block uncached write speed of 65.94 GB/sec. Notably, the instance demonstrates strong performance in text generation for small language models, achieving 282.15 tokens/sec on a 135M model, while maintaining average speeds of 17.54 tokens/sec for medium 7B models.

Given its high memory density and dedicated CPU allocation, the ecs.r7.8xlarge offers a balanced cost-to-resource profile for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. The absence of bundled local storage and GPUs makes it a cost-efficient choice for workloads that do not require hardware acceleration or high-speed local scratch disks. It is well-suited for in-memory databases like Redis, where it achieves 30.13 million operations per second, as well as large-scale web serving, software compilation, and memory-heavy data processing tasks.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8369B processors running at 2.7 GHz on an x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio, but contains no local storage or GPU accelerators. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 16 Gbps. Benchmark results show average performance across general compute and memory tasks, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 13384.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 35326.14. It excels in small-model text generation at 282.15 tokens/sec and handles 30.13 million Redis SET operations per second. This profile makes it highly suitable for memory-intensive workloads such as in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise web serving.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 32 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 256 GB RAM, and 16 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for in-memory databases and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7.8xlarge features 32 dedicated Intel Xeon cores and 256 GB RAM, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and high memory density for demanding database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated Intel Xeon 8369B physical cores running at 2.7 GHz
256.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
16 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with KVM virtualization
Strong performance in small-model LLM text generation at 282.15 tokens/sec
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching layers, and web serving
